texto: I was most happy when pen and paper were taken from me and I was forbidden from doing anything. I had no anxiety about doing nothing by my own fault, my conscience was clear, and I was happy. This was when I was in prison.
explicacao: Citação de Daniil Kharms sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “I was most happy when pen and paper were taken from me and I was forbidden from doing anything. I…” articula felicidade, laziness, prison em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como prison.

frase_original: I was most happy when pen and paper were taken from me and I was forbidden from doing anything. I had no anxiety about doing nothing by my own fault, my conscience was clear, and I was happy. This was when I was in prison.

texto: There lived a redheaded man who had no eyes or ears. He didn’t have hair either, so he was called a redhead arbitrarily. He couldn’t talk because he had no mouth. He had no nose either. He didn’t even have arms or legs. He had no stomach, he had no back, he had no spine, and he had no innards at all. He didn’t have anything. So we don’t even know who we’re talking about. It’s better that we don’t talk about him any more.
explicacao: Citação de Daniil Kharms sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “There lived a redheaded man who had no eyes or ears. He didn’t have hair either, so he was called…” articula reflexao, humorous, either em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: There lived a redheaded man who had no eyes or ears. He didn’t have hair either, so he was called a redhead arbitrarily. He couldn’t talk because he had no mouth. He had no nose either. He didn’t even have arms or legs. He had no stomach, he had no back, he had no spine, and he had no innards at all. He didn’t have anything. So we don’t even know who we’re talking about. It’s better that we don’t talk about him any more.
